name: The Trade Desk Programmatic Advertising API
what_it_does: Enterprise demand-side platform (DSP) with REST API — enabling programmatic advertising across display, video, audio, native, and connected TV (CTV), with data management platform (DMP), audience creation, real-time bidding (RTB), advanced frequency capping, and Unified ID 2.0 for identity-resolved advertising on the open internet.
use_cases: Managing programmatic advertising campaigns across display, video, CTV, and audio via TTD API
Creating and managing first-party audience segments for targeted programmatic campaigns
Pulling campaign performance data (impressions, clicks, conversions) for analytics dashboards
Integrating Unified ID 2.0 for identity-resolved targeting without third-party cookies
Automating bid multipliers and audience exclusions based on performance data via API
not_for: Search advertising (Google/Bing) — TTD focuses on display/video/CTV, not search
Social advertising (Meta/TikTok) — TTD accesses open internet, not walled gardens
Small advertisers (<$75K/month spend) — TTD has high minimum spend requirements
best_when: An enterprise advertiser or agency needs sophisticated programmatic media buying across the open internet, connected TV, and streaming audio — with first-party data activation and cookie-free identity solutions.
avoid_when: You primarily advertise on Meta/Google (walled gardens), are a small-to-mid-market advertiser, or need managed service rather than a technical DSP platform.

auth.notes: API key from TTD Platform UI. TTD-Auth header for all API requests. Partner-scoped and advertiser-scoped access. No webhooks — must poll for reporting data. Reports generated asynchronously — generate report, poll for status, download when ready. Unified ID 2.0 integration for identity resolution. Real-time bidding happens at infrastructure level — API manages campaigns, not RTB.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; minimum spend required
pricing.paid_tiers: Percentage of managed media spend; technology fee typically 10-15% of spend
pricing.notes: Ventura, CA company. Founded 2009. NASDAQ listed (TTD). $1.8B+ revenue (2023). Largest independent DSP globally. CEO Jeff Green. Competing directly with Google's DV360 but independent (no walled garden conflict of interest). Unified ID 2.0 (UID2) is post-cookie identity standard — 500+ publishers. Connected TV (CTV) and streaming audio strength. Solimar platform for AI-powered optimization. OpenPath direct publisher access.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per partner account; enterprise limits available

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for campaigns, ad groups, creatives, audiences, and reporting. TTD-Auth header key auth. Excellent developer documentation. Async reporting model — generate report, poll status, download. No webhooks — polling required. UID2 integration for identity requires separate token server setup. Audience segment creation via API.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Campaign reads fully idempotent. Report reads idempotent. Report generation not idempotent — check existing reports before generating. Audience uploads use batch ID for idempotency.
known_agent_gotchas: Async reporting — generate report request, poll for completion, then download (2-step process)
No webhooks — must implement polling for campaign status and report completion
Minimum spend requirements for direct TTD access — smaller advertisers use managed service
UID2 identity resolution requires separate Operator setup and token management
CTV inventory is premium — much higher CPMs than display; plan budgets accordingly

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2, GDPR, CCPA compliant. US and EU data residency. API key auth. NASDAQ-listed with public company security standards. First-party audience data uploaded to TTD is sensitive — implement data onboarding via secure S3 or SFTP. UID2 token handling requires secure key management. Audience data may include sensitive demographic or behavioral segments.
